content.title: Fig. 1. Timeline of CCTPs implemented in China during 2015-2020. (Note: the public information is collected from the National Energy Administration, and the illustration is made by the authors).
content.summary: Timeline illustrating CCTPs implemented in China from 2015 to 2020, highlighting key initiatives and action plans aimed at the clean and efficient use of coal.
content.insight: The figure outlines major policy and action plans to utilize coal resources cleanly, reduce emissions, and align with China's 13th Five-Year Plan goals, shaping the atmospheric administration strategy.

metadata.full_json: {"id": "img_22", "source_md": "Air quality changes in China 2013-2020_ Effectiven.md", "save_path": "C:\\liuxingyu\\multisubspace-data\\predataprocess\\paper_md\\Air quality changes in China 2013-2020_ Effectiven\\figure_1.jpg", "title": "Fig. 1. Timeline of CCTPs implemented in China during 2015-2020. (Note: the public information is collected from the National Energy Administration, and the illustration is made by the authors).", "references": ["On the policy side, the Chinese government explicitly proposed to promote the clean and efficient use of coal in 2014. And subsequently, two significant specialized action plans were published in 2015 (Action Plan for Clean and Efficient Use of Coal in Industry and Action Plan for Clean and Efficient Use of Coal), signifying that the clean and efficient use of coal in China has entered the implementation stage since 2015 (Zhu, 2019). Herein, the former plan chose four coal-dominated industries as key objects, and defined 21 types of critical technologies for clean and efficient coal utilization in the industry; the latter plan proposed the subjects involved in CCTPs include the government, enterprises, market, and the public, and all of the coal-rich districts in China were selected as key regions. Currently, a series of laws, regulations, and policies have been brought into effect, to alleviate air pollution and accelerate the development of CCTs. Additionally, China's 13th Five-Year Plan (FYP) ranges from 2016 to 2020, which sets goals and directions for national economic and social development during this period. In response to the economic regulation and marker supervision, 2020 is set as the last implementation year of many CCTPs, as shown in Fig. 1. Overall, these policies directed the dominant logic of atmospheric administration in the 13th FYP, namely utilizing coal resources cleanly and efficiently, reducing emissions comprehensively, and decreasing coal consumption."], "functional_roles": {"main_role": "Introduction & Background", "sub_roles": ["Introduce Background Knowledge"]}, "visual_type": {"main_type": "Diagrams & Schematics", "sub_types": ["Flowchart"]}, "key_entities": ["CCTPs", "National Energy Administration", "Action Plan for Clean and Efficient Use of Coal", "13th Five-Year Plan", "Energy Technological Revolution", "Chemical Industry Innovation"], "concise_summary": "Timeline illustrating CCTPs implemented in China from 2015 to 2020, highlighting key initiatives and action plans aimed at the clean and efficient use of coal.", "inferred_insight": "The figure outlines major policy and action plans to utilize coal resources cleanly, reduce emissions, and align with China's 13th Five-Year Plan goals, shaping the atmospheric administration strategy."}

content.title: Fig. 6. The variation trend of AQI in the Chinese mainland.
content.summary: Figure 6 shows the declining trend of AQI from 2013 to 2020 across seven regions in the Chinese mainland, highlighting improved air quality.
content.insight: The figure illustrates significant improvements in air quality after implementing clean coal technology policies, with a notable decrease in AQI values across regions, underscoring the policies' positive impact.

metadata.keywords: ["AQI", "Chinese mainland", "provincial capital cities", "clean coal technology policies", "CCTPs", "central China"]

metadata.full_json: {"id": "img_27", "source_md": "Air quality changes in China 2013-2020_ Effectiven.md", "save_path": "C:\\liuxingyu\\multisubspace-data\\predataprocess\\paper_md\\Air quality changes in China 2013-2020_ Effectiven\\figure_6.jpg", "title": "Fig. 6. The variation trend of AQI in the Chinese mainland.", "references": ["considerably decreased from 2013 to 2020. As shown in Fig. 6, the AQI of all studied cities was all less than 100 since 2017, which is the threshold AQI value of excessive pollutants, indicating that air quality in China began to pick up. Herein, for better comparison, the 31 provincial capital cities were categorized into 7 districts according to the official administrative geographical area division. An identical trend was seen to that in central China, which showed the greatest improvement in air quality during 7 years.", "Clean and efficient use of coal in China entered the implementation stage in 2015, and a series of policies were established to promote the sustainable development of clean coal technologies (CCTs). However, the long-term effects of the clean coal technology policies (CCTPs) have not been evaluated, and the outbreak of coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) would bring great uncertainty regarding the effectiveness evaluation. Employing deep learning approaches and spatial autocorrelation analysis, the present work intended to explore the air quality variation before and after the implementation of CCTPs, and investigate the association between air quality and socioeconomic factors to explore the internal mechanism of air quality improvement. Results showed that after implementing CCTPs, the air quality index (AQI) had an average reduction of  $18.82\\%$  and the identical drop in air pollution in 2018 implied a 2-year time lag of CCTPs. Additionally, emission reduction and deindustrialization were explored as two promising ways to improve air quality while promoting energy transformation. The findings indicated that the Chinese government ought to pay more attention to long-term plans; industrial restructuring and environmental protection should be insisted upon to realize sustainable energy development. By providing a perspective on air quality improvement after the policies are implemented, this study can lead practitioners and academia to a comprehensive and objective view of clean coal policies.", "The study obtained the data including a total of 1440 daily air quality and meteorological data (January 22, 2016, to December 31, 2019) from 31 provincial capital cities in the Chinese mainland,  $60\\%$  of which are chosen as the training set,  $20\\%$  as the validation set and  $20\\%$  as the test set. After determining the best basic network architecture for the current prediction tasks, models are set up in units of cities, and the training set is used to train the tailored CNN-LSTM models. The accuracy indicators of the model prediction results of 31 cities, namely RMSE values averaging 22.06, models perform well overall, indicating that the applied CNN-LSTM network has good capabilities for AQI prediction (Jin et al., 2020; Mao et al., 2021).", "Temporally, it is evident that the AQI in the Chinese mainland", "Fig. 5. AQI regional spatial autocorrelation in 31 provincial cities of the Chinese mainland.", "Fig. 7. The variation trend of AQI in four seasons.", "During the period of CCTPs implementation, an obvious air quality improvement was observed in the Chinese mainland, manifested as a decrease of AQI value by  $18.82\\%$  from 2013 to 2020. It is concluded that the clean and efficient transformation of coal resources conforms to China's national conditions, and could indeed improve the atmospheric environment, which has been demonstrated in the existing research as well (Li and Patino-Echeverri, 2017; Liu et al., 2021). Specifically, correlation coefficients showed that the promotion of emission reduction and deindustrialization could fundamentally help to reduce airborne contamination, although their persistence relies on continued efforts and is influenced by various factors (Yang and Teng, 2018). Another important observation was a 2-year time lag before the stringent CCTPs starting from 2015 takes any strong positive effects, shown as a dramatical reduction of AQI in 2018. It implies that the influence of time lag should be considered in the decision-making process, as a time lag commonly appears in air pollution regulations and plans (Van der et al., 2017).", "The increase in population size and economic activity has remarkably increased the demand for fossil fuels in China, posing a severe threat to the environment. However, the recently implemented coal-based energy transition has shifted the energy structure from polluting energies to environment-friendly energies, and the subsequent policies created a great revolution in energy development and environment protection. Nevertheless, the energy distribution and air pollution in China are characterized by regionality, thus, targeting the efficiency of these national control policies, scholars mainly focus on regions with high-polluting and energy-intensive industries, such as Shanxi province and the BJH region; or target a specific policy, such as CTG and CTE policy. Differently, this study revealed the air quality improvement during the implementation of CCTPs at the national level. Clarifying the internal mechanism of these policies is significant when revising and implementing the following policies to achieve sustainable development. The quantitative assessment provided significant results: i) After implementing CCTPs, the Chinese mainland experienced air quality improvement, with the reduction of AQI averaging  $18.82\\%$ ; ii) An identical reduction of air pollution was observed in 2018, indicating a 2-year time lag of the related control policies; iii) There were positive associations between emission reduction promoting, deindustrialization and air quality improvement, suggesting two promising ways to realize sustainable energy development. Through assessing the air quality changes before and after the implementation of CCTPs, it is expected to provide a scientific basis for policymakers and stakeholders to forge consensus on the establishment and enforcement of clean coal policies."], "functional_roles": {"main_role": "Results & Findings", "sub_roles": ["Present Core Findings", "Present Quantitative Results"]}, "visual_type": {"main_type": "Quantitative Charts", "sub_types": ["Line Chart"]}, "key_entities": ["AQI", "Chinese mainland", "provincial capital cities", "clean coal technology policies", "CCTPs", "central China"], "concise_summary": "Figure 6 shows the declining trend of AQI from 2013 to 2020 across seven regions in the Chinese mainland, highlighting improved air quality.", "inferred_insight": "The figure illustrates significant improvements in air quality after implementing clean coal technology policies, with a notable decrease in AQI values across regions, underscoring the policies' positive impact."}

content.title: Fig. 2. Heating area and coal consumption (1997-2018).
content.summary: Fig. 2 depicts a timeline of policies related to air pollution control and energy transition from coal to cleaner alternatives in Northern China from 1963 to 2021.
content.insight: The figure illustrates the progressive implementation of domestic and foreign policies aimed at reducing coal consumption and improving air quality, culminating in the 'Winter Clean Heating Plan' for 2017-2021.

{"id": "img_69", "source_md": "Does energy transition improve air quality_ Eviden.md", "save_path": "C:\liuxingyu\multisubspace-data\predataprocess\paper_md\Does energy transition improve air quality_ Eviden\figure_2.jpg", "title": "Fig. 2. Heating area and coal consumption (1997-2018).", "references": ["Due to the lower temperatures in Northern China, burning coal for heating had become the most common way in which residents kept warm in winter. Since the 1950s, the Government of China provided winter heating via coal-fired boilers in the north Huai River areas. Northern cities received free unlimited heating from November 15 to March 15. Up until now, heating has not only been an important indicator of social wealth but has also become necessary for the survival of northern people in winter. According to statistical data released by the Ministry of Housing and Urban-Rural Development (MOHURD) of the People's Republic of China, the annual growth rate of the urban centralized heating area reached  $14.04\%$  during 1991-2017 (Fig. 2). At the same time, coal consumption increased under the rising demand for heating in the two decades prior to 2013. The "coal-to-gas/electricity" policy has generally altered the previous energy consumption structure. However, the rural heating energy consumption structure has largely remained unbalanced, among which coal consumption accounts for approximately  $75\%$ . Moreover, the large scattered coal heating demand has caused a series of environmental and social problems. The combustion of coal in boilers is associated with the release of air pollutants, especially  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  and total suspended particles (TSP). Winter heating has also reduced people's life expectancy in northern regions of China [27]. To meet the increasing demand for heating as well as to alleviate air pollutant emissions, the National Development and Reform Commission of the People's Republic of China announced its plan for the WCHP project (covering 2017-2021).", "implementing intervention measures have seen substantial air quality improvements [7,8]. For example, the United States of America has replaced coal with natural gas in generating electric power since 1997 and has achieved  $44\%$  and  $40\%$  reduction in  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  and  $\mathrm{NO}_{\mathrm{x}}$  emissions from the 1997 levels [9]. A cleaner heating technologies program in New Zealand has led to the significant decline of PM emissions and concentrations [10]. Such policies aiming to diversify energy structures with clean and renewable energy have been successful in certain countries, which also has important implications for China [11]. In Northern China, the annual consumption of coal used for heating is approximately 400 million metric tons, nearly  $50\%$  of which is separately distributed for use in rural areas. Moreover, the combustion of scattered coal heating has caused severe haze to occur in winter [12]. Chen et al. found that the combustion of scattered coal heating has led to an approximately two-thirds increase in air pollutants during periods when heating is employed (i.e., winter) compared to periods when heating is not employed (i.e., summer) [13]. Compared to coal, clean energies such as natural gas and wind power emit less  $\mathrm{SO}_2$ ,  $\mathrm{NO}_{\mathrm{x}}$ ,  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ , and  $\mathrm{PM}_{10}$ , and thus reduce human health risks from exposure to air pollutants [9]. Therefore, switching from scattered coal", "Studies on the effectiveness of energy transition have not produced a consensus among scholars. Lin and Zhu [18] found that fuel switching in "energy saving and emission reduction" (ESER) policy had failed to decrease  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations. Li and Chen [19] reported that a city that underwent coal-to-gas transition had no significant impact on reducing  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  emissions, and this was primarily because the city focused on the replacement of industrial coal while ignoring the combustion of scattered coal heating in rural areas. Additionally, although Zhao and Luo [20] reported that a "coal-to-gas" project decreased pollutant emissions from a household sector by substituting civil urban coal consumption, they confirmed that this project had a limited effect on reducing industrial coal consumption. Liu et al. [21] adopted the GAINS model to examine the effect of coal switching strategy in Jing-Jin-Ji region, and estimated that the primary  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  and  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  decreased by  $28\%$  and  $11\%$ , respectively. Wu et al. [22] evaluated the household coal-replacement program in North China and concluded that coal-to-electricity policy achieved the goal of a clean winter, while the high-quality coal replacement policy had no improvement in indoor air quality. Since the  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  (smog) crisis in the winter of 2013, environmental governance in China has shifted attention to scattered coal heating in rural areas, after which a series of policies were implemented and subsequently achieved remarkable results [23]. However, official reports may have overestimated the benefits of the clean heating policy by simply attributing the decrease in air pollutant concentrations to the promotion of clean heating policies while ignoring the interference of other exogenous energy-saving and emission-reduction policies [24]. Additionally, policy impacts will vary in different regions; thus, it is necessary to identify the validity of such policy among regions. Few studies to date have focused on the evaluation of energy transition policies in China. As an important part of China's energy transition process, it must be determined that whether or not the "Winter Clean Heating Pilot" (WCHP) project improves air quality. Answering this question can offer some guidance in China's future environmental governance while also providing a quantitative reference for other coal-based, energy-consuming developing countries. It could also be beneficial to achieve the synergy between emission reduction and air quality improvement under the background of climate change.", "On the one hand, even though the WCHP project has proven to be an effective mean to control winter-derived heating emissions, the cost of this project should not be ignored. The conversion cost of "coal-to-gas/electricity" is higher and includes a subsequent increase in the cost of gas. Replacing unprocessed coal with clean coal can be used as a transitional method in the ultimate goal of energy transition for regions that experience considerable financial governmental pressure and low residential income. The government should focus on reforming the cost of electricity/gas by updating to more advanced heating technology and devices. On the other hand, since the reduction effect of the WCHP project on regional air quality varies under different development levels and resource distributions, project control should be delegated to regional governments based on local features rather than following the outdated pattern of "one-size-fits-all". In order to minimize the negative impact of clean heating policies on energy poverty and energy consumption inequality, the government should provide subsidies to clean energy more affordable to low-income groups.", "[1] Pope lii CA, Burnett RT, Thun MJ, Calle EE, Krewski D, Ito K, et al. content.title: Fig. 3. Key milestones and policy evolution of air pollution control in China, including control actions on the emissions of  SO_2  and  NO_x  and on ambient concentrations of  PM_{2.5} . FYP: Five-Year Plan.
content.summary: The figure depicts the timeline and milestones in China's air pollution control policies, focusing on SO2, NOx emissions, and PM2.5 concentrations, highlighting significant control actions and achievements.

Such progress could not have been achieved without innovative improvement in political accountability, verification of  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  emissions, and financial incentives for power plants to install and operate flue gas desulfurization (FGD).  $\mathrm{NO}_x$  has more sources than  $\mathrm{SO}_2$ , including coal burning, mobile sources, and other fossil fuel combustion sectors. content.title: Fig. 4. Summary of the major control regulations in China since 2013. PP: power plants; IN: industry; TR: transport; DO: domestic sector; AR: agriculture.
content.summary: The table summarizes China's major control regulations for PM2.5 mitigation from 2013 to 2020, highlighting different action plans and targeted emission sectors.
content.insight: The figure illustrates the shift in China's policy framework, gradually encompassing more emission sectors and precursors to achieve substantial PM2.5 reductions nationwide, emphasizing extensive measures in key regions.

One notable case is that the estimated  $\\mathrm{SO}_2$  emissions and sulfate concentrations inversed from satellite observations both rebounded slightly during 2010-2011 due to a rapid increase in industrial activities and less stringent controls on the iron and steel industries (Fig. 2), which surpassed the reduction benefits of  $\\mathrm{SO}_2$  controls on power plants [31]. As a result, a series of stringent control measures have been implemented in the industrial polluting sector, including strengthening industrial emissions standards, phasing out small-scale high-polluting factories, phasing out outdated industrial capacities, and upgrading industrial boilers [32]. Compared with the control measures for", "As Fig. 4 indicates, control actions have been implemented nationwide, with additional efforts in several key regions. Improving air quality and mitigating  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  pollution in core megacities (e.g., Beijing and Shanghai) have been attained due to increased attention from the central government and the public. Beijing provides a success story in tackling  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  pollution after two-decade-long efforts. In fact, the implementation of comprehensive air pollution controls in Beijing was initiated as early as the 1990s. During the 2008 Olympic Games, extensive control measures were implemented in Beijing and surrounding provinces to guarantee good air quality [41]. Ambient concentrations of CO,  $\\mathrm{SO}_2$ ,  $\\mathrm{PM}_{10}$ , and  $\\mathrm{NO}_2$  continuously decreased, even as the municipal GDP, resident population, vehicle stock, and energy consumption increased significantly from 1998 to 2013. content.title: Fig. 5. AQI regional spatial autocorrelation in 31 provincial cities of the Chinese mainland.
content.summary: The figure displays annual spatial autocorrelation of AQI in 31 Chinese provincial cities from 2013 to 2020, highlighting high-high and low-low pollution areas.
content.insight: The figure illustrates regional AQI patterns, showing reduced pollution in northern China post-policy implementation, supporting the effectiveness of air quality improvements.

metadata.image_path: C:\liuxingyu\multisubspace-data\predataprocess\paper_md\Air quality changes in China 2013-2020_ Effectiven\figure_5.jpg
metadata.full_json: {"id": "img_26", "source_md": "Air quality changes in China 2013-2020_ Effectiven.md", "save_path": "C:\\liuxingyu\\multisubspace-data\\predataprocess\\paper_md\\Air quality changes in China 2013-2020_ Effectiven\\figure_5.jpg", "title": "Fig. 5. AQI regional spatial autocorrelation in 31 provincial cities of the Chinese mainland.", "references": ["After the spatial gatherings of air pollution are confirmed, to better identify the gathering district, a regional spatial autocorrelation analysis is also conducted. As shown in Fig. 5, there were still high-high gathering areas (high levels of pollution) and low-low gathering areas (low levels of pollution) from 2013 to 2020. Meanwhile, a north-south spatial heterogeneity pattern was obvious, depicting the high levels of pollution in northern China and low levels of pollution in southern China.", "Clean and efficient use of coal in China entered the implementation stage in 2015, and a series of policies were established to promote the sustainable development of clean coal technologies (CCTs). However, the long-term effects of the clean coal technology policies (CCTPs) have not been evaluated, and the outbreak of coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) would bring great uncertainty regarding the effectiveness evaluation. Employing deep learning approaches and spatial autocorrelation analysis, the present work intended to explore the air quality variation before and after the implementation of CCTPs, and investigate the association between air quality and socioeconomic factors to explore the internal mechanism of air quality improvement. Results showed that after implementing CCTPs, the air quality index (AQI) had an average reduction of  $18.82\\%$  and the identical drop in air pollution in 2018 implied a 2-year time lag of CCTPs. Additionally, emission reduction and deindustrialization were explored as two promising ways to improve air quality while promoting energy transformation. The findings indicated that the Chinese government ought to pay more attention to long-term plans; industrial restructuring and environmental protection should be insisted upon to realize sustainable energy development. By providing a perspective on air quality improvement after the policies are implemented, this study can lead practitioners and academia to a comprehensive and objective view of clean coal policies.", "To accelerate energy transformation and reduce air pollution, both central and provincial governments in China are taking increasingly leading roles in shaping policy outcomes, through adopting cleaner production or energy saving policies, and revisions of local standards or emission reduction. Since the Chinese energy structure is characterized by being rich in coal, poor in oil, and lack of gas, thus a series of coal-togas (CTG) and coal-to-electricity (CTE) policies are established to promote the clean conversion of coal resources. Further, coal-fired stoves were the most popular source of heating in winter in Northern China, which are cheap and easily available, however, would lead to heavy emissions of various air contaminants (Fan et al., 2020). Strategies to control residential coal combustion (RCC) have been a focus since 2016. Accordingly, there is growing attention from researchers to examine the effectiveness of RCC, CTG, and CTE policies (Xue et al., 2016; Chen and Chen, 2019; Chen et al., 2022). Applying a comprehensive perspective of economic, energy, and environmental impact, Lin and Jia (2020) researched that the CTE policy is an effective pattern to eliminate  $\\mathrm{SO}_2$  and  $\\mathrm{NO}_{\\mathrm{x}}$  emissions, more than half of residents' emissions could be cut down, and the co-benefit between CTE and other energy saving policies was observed. As for the implementation of the RCC policy, Li et al. (2018) estimated that air quality in Beijing would be remarkably improved if residential coal in the BTH region and the surrounding areas are replaced by other clean energy sources. Similar results have been obtained in Xue et al.'s research (2016) as well, using scenario analysis, the projected emissions of primary pollutants from RCC showed that it is difficult to eliminate the emissions from residential sources only rely on mandatory control measures, adopting multiple kinds of policy instruments to promote the clean conversion of coal resources is a preferable pathway (Xue et al., 2016). Yu et al. (2021) examined the dynamic effects of CTG policy and its impact mechanism on air pollution, revealing that this policy has significantly improved air quality in 274 Chinese prefecture-level cities, with a reduction of  $31.3\\%$  for industrial  $\\mathrm{SO}_2$ ,  $36\\%$  for industrial smoke, and  $33.1\\%$  for AQI.", "The Chinese mainland is composed of 33 administrative regions, however, the policies implemented by Hong Kong and Macao special administrative regions differ from the other provinces. Moreover, coal-dominated factories and enterprises are mainly located in the capital city of these provinces, which are typical epitomes of regional environmental pollution. Therefore, a total of 31 provincial capital cities in the Chinese mainland are selected. The system architecture of this study is depicted in Fig. 2.", "To further analyze the temporal and spatial variation features of air quality in 31 provincial capital cities from 2013 to 2020, the spatial autocorrelation analysis method is applied. ArcGIS 10.6 and GeoDa software is used for the calculation and analytic process.", "The study obtained the data including a total of 1440 daily air quality and meteorological data (January 22, 2016, to December 31, 2019) from 31 provincial capital cities in the Chinese mainland,  $60\\%$  of which are chosen as the training set,  $20\\%$  as the validation set and  $20\\%$  as the test set. After determining the best basic network architecture for the current prediction tasks, models are set up in units of cities, and the training set is used to train the tailored CNN-LSTM models. The accuracy indicators of the model prediction results of 31 cities, namely RMSE values averaging 22.06, models perform well overall, indicating that the applied CNN-LSTM network has good capabilities for AQI prediction (Jin et al., 2020; Mao et al., 2021).", "Global spatial autocorrelation analysis is carried out using the average monthly AQIs for the 31 studied cities from 2013 to 2020. As shown in Table 3, the results of global Moran's I are all greater than 0 and statistically significant, indicating that there are significant and positive spatial correlations among the average monthly AQIs. In summary, there is an obvious regional integration tendency of air pollution in Chinese cities."], "functional_roles": {"main_role": "Results & Findings", "sub_roles": ["Present Core Findings"]}, "visual_type": {"main_type": "Geospatial Visualizations", "sub_types": ["Map"]}, "key_entities": ["AQI", "spatial autocorrelation", "Chinese mainland", "provincial cities"], "concise_summary": "The figure displays annual spatial autocorrelation of AQI in 31 Chinese provincial cities from 2013 to 2020, highlighting high-high and low-low pollution areas.", "inferred_insight": "The figure illustrates regional AQI patterns, showing reduced pollution in northern China post-policy implementation, supporting the effectiveness of air quality improvements."}

content.title: Fig. 7. Major air pollution control goals, policies and specific control measures in China from the end of 2013 to 2020.
content.summary: The figure outlines China's major air pollution control goals, policies, and measures from 2013 to 2020, focusing on process reduction, structure reduction, and source reduction.
content.insight: The measures effectively decreased PM2.5 concentrations and associated premature deaths but highlighted the need for targeted strategies and mitigation measures for sustained benefits.

metadata.full_json:
{"id": "img_135", "source_md": "How the Air Clean Plan and carbon mitigation measu.md", "save_path": "C:\liuxingyu\multisubspace-data\predataprocess\paper_md\How the Air Clean Plan and carbon mitigation measu\figure_7.jpg", "title": "Fig. 7. Major air pollution control goals, policies and specific control measures in China from the end of 2013 to 2020.", "references": ["The control goals, policies, and specific measures in China during ACP are characterized in Fig. 7. Specifically, for the source control, a series of stringent industry emission standards were enacted to limit energy and industrial emissions (e.g., thermal power, iron and steel, and cement) for controlling energy consumption. Regarding the process control, clean production (e.g., using end-of-pipe devices in coal-fired power plants) was the major measure. In addition, implementation of ultra-low emission transformation of steel and other industries was advocated in APWBSD. The  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  emissions per coal and energy", "China implemented a stringent Air Clean Plan (ACP) since 2013 to address environmental and health risks caused by ambient fine particulate matter  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ . However, the policy effectiveness of ACP and co-benefits of carbon mitigation measures to environment and health are still largely unknown. Using satellite-based  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  products produced in our previous study, concentration-response functions, and the logarithmic mean Divisia index (LMDI) method, we analyzed the spatiotemporal dynamics of premature deaths attributable to  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  exposure, and quantitatively estimated the policy benefits of ACP and carbon mitigation measures. We found the annual  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations in China decreased by  $33.65\%$ $(13.41\mu \mathrm{g}\mathrm{m}^{-3})$  from 2014 to 2020, accompanied by a decrease in  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ -attributable premature deaths of 0.23 million  $(95\%$  confidence interval (CI): 0.22-0.27), indicating the huge benefits of China ACP for human health and environment. However, there were still 1.12 million  $(95\%$  CI: 0.79-1.56) premature deaths caused by the exposure of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  in mainland China in 2020. Among all ACP measures, clean production (contributed  $55.98\%$  and  $51.14\%$  to decrease in  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  and premature deaths attributable to  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ ) and energy consumption control (contributed  $32.58\%$  and  $29.54\%$  to decrease in  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  and premature deaths attributable to  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ ) made the largest contribution during the past seven years. Nevertheless, the environmental and health benefits of ACP are not fully synergistic in different regions, and the effectiveness of ACP measures reduced from 2018 to 2020. The co-effects of  $\mathrm{CO}_{2}$  and  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  has become one of the major drivers for  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  and premature deaths reduction since 2018, confirming the clear environment and health co-benefits of carbon mitigation measures. Our study suggests, with the saturation of clean production and source control, more targeted region-specific strategies and synergistic air pollution-carbon mitigation measures are critical to achieving the WHO's Air Quality Guideline target and the UN's Sustainable Development Goal Target in China.", "Therefore, the Chinese government launched the strict Air Clean Plan (ACP) in 2013, aiming to address the above problem of severe air pollution. The ACP was divided into two phases: a five-year Air Pollution Prevention and Control Action Plan (APPCAP, 2013-2017) as well as a three-year Action Plan to Win Blue-Sky Defense (APWBSD, 2018-2020) (The State Council of China, 2013; 2018). The APPCAP was targeted to decrease  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations by over  $25\%$ ,  $20\%$ , and  $15\%$  by 2017 in the Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei (BTH), Yangtze River Delta (YRD) and Pearl River Delta (PRD) regions, respectively (Fig. S1 and Table S1). The APWBSD, launched in 2018, set more comprehensive air quality requirements, such as the requirement that  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations needed to be reduced by over  $18\%$  compared to 2015 in all cities at prefecture-level where the standard was not met. In this context, a series of effective air pollution control measures were proposed on energy consumption control, clean production and energy structure adjustment (Zhang et al., 2019a; Zheng et al., 2018). However, greater effort remains necessary and urgent to reduce the  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations to the WHO level and the standard of "Beautiful China" vision (annual mean  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations  $\leq 35~\mu \mathrm{g}~\mathrm{m}^{-3}$  in all cities by 2035).", "Although some pioneering research has explored improvements in air quality and health impacts due to the ACP (Yue et al., 2020; Geng et al., 2021; Zhang et al., 2019a; Liu et al., 2021; Feng et al., 2017; Li et al., 2019; Zhu et al., 2019), these studies suffered from several limitations. First, current estimates of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ -attributable premature deaths are limited in both spatial resolution and temporal scale. For example, Zhang et al. (2019a) estimated the change in  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ -attributable premature deaths in China from 2013 to 2017, but their analysis was limited to the national level. Yue et al. (2020) estimated pixel level premature deaths attributable to  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  exposure. However, the relative coarse spatial resolution (10 km) made it difficult to discern detailed information clearly such as hot spots of severe regions. Second, few previous studies focused on the environment and health benefits of specific measures for  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  pollution control in the ACP in China (Cai et al., 2017; Huang et al., 2018; Ma et al., 2019; Ding et al., 2019). Zheng et al. (2018) estimated the effectiveness of ACP by measuring the related induced decrease of emitted pollutants (e.g.,  $\mathrm{SO}_2$ ,  $\mathrm{NO_x}$  and  $\mathrm{NH_3}$ ). Geng et al. (2021) considered the effectiveness of end-of-pipe treatment in ACP, while ignoring the effect of other specific measures in ACP. Finally, carbon mitigation and  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  control have significant synergies (Li et al., 2019; Cheng et al., 2021; Xing et al., 2020; Tong et al., 2021). However, currently only a few studies considered or quantified the co-benefits of  $\mathrm{CO}_{2}$  mitigation and reducing  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  so far. Some other studies projected", "Fig. 3 shows the population exposed to ambient  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  in pollution control priority regions and in the whole nation in specific years at the beginning and end of the two phases of ACP (2014, 2017, and 2020). In 2014, over  $80\%$  of Chinese people lived in areas with  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  over  $35\mu \mathrm{g}$ $\mathrm{m}^{-3}$  (WHO IT-1). In 2020, the corresponding proportion decreased to  $40\%$ . BTH had the most severe  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  exposure, even with the most prominent air quality improvement amongst four priority regions. In 2014, almost all populations in BTH lived in areas where  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations exceeded the WHO IT-1 threshold and this population proportion decreased to  $90\%$  in 2020. The FWP was not considered as a priority region for air pollution control in APPCAP, and pollution status was more serious during APPCAP as a result of crude industrial production and its gully terrain that was not conducive to the diffusion of pollutants. By contrast, the effect of ACP measures was profound in FWP region under APWBSD with a  $10\%$  reduction in the population exposed to  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5} > 35\mu \mathrm{g}\mathrm{m}^{-3}$  in 2020 compared to 2017. The exposed population in PRD is relatively low compared to other priority regions, with  $24\%$  meeting the WHO IT-1 in 2014 and  $100\%$  meeting the WHO IT-1 in 2020, which also achieved the "Beautiful China" vision. However, almost all populations ( $\sim 98\%$  in the whole nation) still lived under the  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations exceeding the WHO IT-3 in 2020, not to mention the WHO AQG standard.", "We provide a comprehensive estimation of the  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations and the number of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ -attributable premature deaths, as well as spatiotemporal changes in these, and a quantitative assessment of the effectiveness of ACP methods in mainland China during 2014 to 2020. The two key contributions are: (1) We estimated the spatiotemporal variation in the number of deaths attributable to  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  exposure in mainland China during ACP based on the  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  product with the finest spatial resolution available to date, and elucidated the spatial discrepancies in policy implementation across China. China's recent clean air actions have achieved great benefits for human health and the environment. However, over  $98\%$  of the population still lived under the risk of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  pollution ( $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations over  $15\mu \mathrm{g}\mathrm{m}^{-3}$ ), with 1.12 million ( $95\%$  CI: 0.79-1.56) premature deaths caused by  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  pollution in mainland China in 2020, accounting for  $\sim 27.05\%$  of global ambient  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ -attributable premature deaths. In addition, the environmental and health benefits of China ACP are not fully synergistic in spatial pattern, with the greatest health benefits detected in the YRD region and the best environment benefits identified in the BTH region. To achieve the co-benefits of environment and health, specific policies need to be adopted under different conditions. (2) We quantified the environment and health co-benefits of ACP measures and carbon mitigation from 2014 to 2020. Clean production was the most effective abatement measure in China ACP, reducing net  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations and net premature deaths by  $18.35\mu \mathrm{g}\mathrm{m}^{-3}$  and 0.45 million, respectively. The co-effects of  $\mathrm{CO}_{2}$  and  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  contributed to a  $23\%$  and  $26\%$  decrease in the number of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  and premature deaths and became one of the major drivers for  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  and premature deaths reduction in the APWBSD phase. This confirms the clear environment and health co-benefits of carbon mitigation measures. Meanwhile, the effectiveness of process and source control measures reduced during 2018 to 2020. With the exhaustion of end-of-pipe and energy consumption control measures, the future air clean actions should focus more on carbon abatement and energy structure optimization, especially under the background of global climate change mitigation. In addition to mitigation in industrial and energy sectors, it is also critical to explore more cost-effective approaches (e.g., agricultural ammonia mitigation) (Zhou et al., 2021; Gu et al., 2021) for pollution control in other sectors.", "Our analysis of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  attributed deaths in China was compared with several recent studies (Table S3). Great progresses was made in previous studies (Zhang et al., 2019a; Li et al., 2019; Ding et al., 2019; McDuffie et al., 2021; Liu et al., 2017; Zou et al., 2019; Apte et al., 2015), albeit with several limitations in common: (1) previous studies focused mainly on the APPCAP (2013-2017) and lack the updated results of the APWBSD (2018-2020); (2) the chemical transport model was used widely in previous research, which showed advantages for predicting the  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations without ground measurement, but also has limitations in terms of prediction accuracy (Ma et al., 2022); (3) the Integrated Exposure Response (IER) model used for premature death estimation in previous studies lacked sufficient information on deaths at high  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  exposure, and failed to separate exposure from ambient  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  and secondhand smoke, which could potentially introduce uncertainties (Burnett et al., 2014). Our research provides several advances compared to previous studies. First, we quantified the co-benefit of the Air Clean Plan and carbon mitigation measures in China for the environment and health from 2014 to 2020. Second, we used state-of-the-art  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  products with the highest accuracy (Wang et al., 2021; 2022) (Fig. S3 and Fig. S4 for detail) and the finest resolution (spatial resolution of  $1\mathrm{km}$ , Fig. S2) at the national scale to offer a full-coverage estimation of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ -attributable premature deaths. With these innovations, we not only provided a more accurate estimation of the risk due to exposure to  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ , but also to analyze the great spatially heterogeneity of policy effectiveness. Third, we adopted the updated MR-BRT spline method, which fits the risk with a more flexible shape for more reliable estimation of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ -attributable premature deaths (Hystad et al., 2020). The MR-BRT includes more exposure scenarios at high  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  levels during fitting compared with other models such as the IER model (Yusuf et al., 2020; Chowdhury et al., 2022). It also excludes active smoking to control for and reduce potential bias due to this effect (Pope et al., 2018). Finally, our study estimated the co-benefit from  $\mathrm{CO}_{2}$  and  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  to the environment and health under the background of the ACP and NDC pledges for the first time.", "Our study estimated the changes in  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentration and premature deaths attributable to  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  in China at a fine-resolution (1 km) during 2014-2020, identifying the measure with greatest environmental and health co-benefits in ACP, and demonstrating the potential of carbon mitigation measures for air quality improvement and public health enhancement. Results showed that (1) the average  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations in China decreased by  $33.65\%$  ( $13.41~\mu \mathrm{g}~\mathrm{m}^{-3}$ ) from 2014 to 2020, accompanied by a decrease in total attributed deaths of 0.23 million (95 % CI: 0.22-0.27), indicating the great benefits of China ACP for human health and environment. However, the premature deaths caused by  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  pollution in mainland China still reached 1.12 million (95 % CI: 0.79-1.56) in 2020. (2) Among China ACP measures, clean production and energy consumption control made the largest contribution to air quality improvements and health burden mitigation during the past seven years. However, the effectiveness of the two ACP measures reduced during 2018 to 2020. Meanwhile, the environmental and health benefits of China ACP are not fully synergistic spatially. (3) The co-effects of  $\mathrm{CO}_{2}$  and  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  has become one of the major drivers for  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  and  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ -attributable premature deaths reduction in the APWBSD phase, confirming the clear environment and health co-benefits of carbon mitigation measures.", "Anenberg, S.C., et al., 2010. evaluation.extracted_insight: 图表描述了中国从2013年到2020年主要空气污染控制目标、政策和具体控制措施，强调PM2.5浓度及早亡减少的有效性。

content.title: Fig. 7. The variation trend of AQI in four seasons.
content.summary: Fig. 7 illustrates the AQI change rate across four seasons in various Chinese regions, highlighting significant decreases in winter.
content.insight: The figure demonstrates how AQI variations are most pronounced in winter, with notable declines in northeast China, reflecting the impact of CCTPs.

The variation trend of AQI in four seasons.", "references": ["To conduct a thorough analysis of AQI variations based on seasonal patterns, the average AQI change rate in four seasons was computed, as shown in Fig. 7. The four seasons were divided based on meteorology, where spring ranges from March to May, summer from June to August, autumn from September to November, and winter from December to February of the next year. Results showed that the most significant decrease in AQI occurred in winter, with average rates of  $35.46\\%$ ,  $30.23\\%$ ,  $29.22\\%$ ,  $25.90\\%$ ,  $24.25\\%$ ,  $22.37\\%$  and  $6.63\\%$  for the northeast, southern, central, eastern, northern, southwest, and northwest China, respectively. The AQI decrease in autumn was also substantial, and northeast China had the greatest decline, averaging  $35.77\\%$  after the implementation of CCTPs. The AQI change rate in summer and spring was less than that of the other two seasons, with an average of  $15.81\\%$  and  $13.41\\%$ , respectively.", "Fig. 6. The variation trend of AQI in the Chinese mainland."], "functional_roles": {"main_role": "Results & Findings", "sub_roles": ["Present Quantitative Results", "Present Core Findings"]}, "visual_type": {"main_type": "Quantitative Charts", "sub_types": ["Bar Chart"]}, "key_entities": ["AQI", "seasonal patterns", "northeast China", "CCTPs", "meteorology"], "concise_summary": "Fig. 7 illustrates the AQI change rate across four seasons in various Chinese regions, highlighting significant decreases in winter.", "inferred_insight": "The figure demonstrates how AQI variations are most pronounced in winter, with notable declines in northeast China, reflecting the impact of CCTPs."}

content.title: Fig.4 Number of industrial heat source objects and industrial heat anomaly points in Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei and its surrounding areas
content.summary: The figure illustrates the trend in numbers of industrial heat source objects and industrial heat anomaly points in Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei from 2015 to 2020, showing a decrease over time.
content.insight: The figure suggests a decline in industrial heat sources and anomalies, reflecting effective policy implementation and industrial transformation efforts in the Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei region.

{"id": "img_223", "source_md": "一种工业热源提取方法_利用温度特征模板的BP神经网络.md", "save_path": "C:\liuxingyu\multisubspace-data\predataprocess\paper_md\一种工业热源提取方法_利用温度特征模板的BP神经网络\figure_4.jpg", "title": "Fig.4 Number of industrial heat source objects and industrial heat anomaly points in Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei and its surrounding areas", "supplement": "(a) 工业热源对象 (b) 工业热源热异常点 图4 京津冀及周边地区工业热源对象和工业热异常点数量 ", "references": ["Abstract: Aiming at the problem of insufficient quantity and spatial refinement in the extraction of industrial heat source from annual scale thermal anomaly data, a neural network industrial heat source extraction method based on temperature feature template is proposed by using VIIRS active fire data. This study took Beijing-Tianjin- Hebei and its surrounding areas as the study area, Firstly, according to the spatial aggregation characteristics of industrial heat sources, the heat source objects were divided by the OPTICS algorithm. Secondly, according to the thermal radiation characteristics of the heat sources, the temperature characteristic template of industrial heat sources and non- industrial heat sources were constructed. Finally, the BP neural network was used to extract industrial heat source objects using the temperature feature template and heat source statistical characteristics as parameters. The results show that: (1) the extraction precision of industrial heat source of the neural network algorithm of temperature feature template proposed in this paper reached  $96.31\%$ . Compared with time filtering and logistic regression methods, the extraction precision of industrial heat sources was improved by  $8.45\%$  and  $7.53\%$ , respectively; (2) From 2015 to 2020, the number of industrial heat sources in the six provinces and cities in Beijing-Tianjin- Hebei and its surrounding areas decreased by  $27.46\%$ . The number of industrial heat source objects and heat anomalies in Hebei Province decreased by  $8.06\%$  and  $7.44\%$  annually, respectively, which was the largest decrease compared with other provinces and cities. The", "concentration of industrial heat sources in Shandong and Tianjin increased by  $25.72\%$  and  $86.64\%$ , respectively, indicating that the industrial transformation and upgrade policies in the two places have achieved remarkable results; (3) Tangshan, Handan, Lvliang, and Changzhi accounted for  $31.37\%$  of the total industrial heat sources in the study area, which are the main cities in Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei and its surrounding areas. The degree of industrial heat source accumulation and energy consumption in seven cities such as Linfen and Taiyuan was higher than those in other cities; The degree of industrial heat source accumulation and energy consumption in 11 cities such as Beijing and Zhoukou was lower than those in other cities; (4) From January to May 2020, the number of industrial heat anomalies in Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei and its surrounding areas remained unchanged or increased compared with the same period in 2019 and 2021. The COVID-19 had no significant impact on the industrial heat source in the study area. The number of industrial heat anomalies in Wuhan in January and February 2020 decreased by more than  $66.67\%$  compared with that in the same period in 2019 and 2021, the number of industrial heat anomalies from March to May 2020 was lower than that in the same period of 2019. The COVID-19 has had a significant impact on industrial heat sources in Wuhan from January to May 2020. This study reflects the current situation and trend of industrial heat source development in Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei and its surrounding areas, which provides a valuable reference for the formulation and adjustment of relevant policies such as reducing energy consumption and improving secondary industry concentration.", "Key words: industrial heat source; OPTICS algorithm; temperature feature template; neural network; VIIRS Active Fire; Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei and its surrounding areas", "(d) Slope_NFHWH

content.title: Fig. 2. Schematic diagram of the monitoring site in the East Yan'an Road Tunnel, Shanghai.
content.summary: The figure displays the location and setup of air pollution monitoring sites within East Yan'an Road Tunnel, Shanghai. It includes schematic representations of the tunnel and monitoring locations.
content.insight: The schematic provides spatial context for the air quality monitoring study, highlighting the strategic placement of sensors to capture key pollutants from vehicle emissions in a high-traffic urban tunnel.

metadata.full_json:
{"id": "img_95", "source_md": "Evaluation of emission factors for light-duty gaso.md", "save_path": "C:\\liuxingyu\\multisubspace-data\\predataprocess\\paper_md\\Evaluation of emission factors for light-duty gaso\\figure_2.jpg", "title": "Fig. 2. Schematic diagram of the monitoring site in the East Yan'an Road Tunnel, Shanghai.", "references": ["A tunnel in the city center of Shanghai $(31.24^{\\circ}\\mathrm{N}, 121.50^{\\circ}\\mathrm{E})$ was selected to conduct air pollution monitoring. The tunnel is called East Yan'an Road tunnel, is an underwater tunnel crossing the Huangpu River in Shanghai. It has two bores with two lanes for each direction. The total length is $2,107\\mathrm{m}$ ; cross-sectional area is $33.75\\mathrm{m}^2$ . To continuously monitor major air pollutants from vehicle exhaust, we installed two integrated air pollution monitoring units at $150\\mathrm{m}$ away from the inlet and outlet of the tunnel, respectively. The height of the unit was about $1.2\\mathrm{m}$ . Fig. 2 shows the location of the tunnel and the monitoring sites. There were 4 sensors which could separately monitor NO, $\\mathrm{NO}_2$ , CO, and $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ concentrations in each unit. NO, $\\mathrm{NO}_2$ , and CO were detected using electrochemical method. Their detection limits were $0.01\\mathrm{mg}\\mathrm{m}^{-3}$ , $0.01\\mathrm{mg}\\mathrm{m}^{-3}$ , and $0.15\\mathrm{mg}\\mathrm{m}^{-3}$ , respectively. $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ was detected by light scattering method. Its resolution was $0.1\\mu \\mathrm{g}\\mathrm{m}^{-3}$ . The monitoring data was continuously transmitted to a remote database through wireless network. The system was built in July 2016 and has been operated by now. To ensure the reliability of monitoring", "Vehicle flow and average speed in the tunnel were detected by a buried induction coil and transmitted to a data platform in real time. The fleet compositions were recognized by automatic acquisition of vehicle license plate information through a high-definition video in the tunnel. Fig. 4 shows the average traffic flow and fleet composition in each hour of weekday and weekend. The data between August and November of 2016 were used in this study. Daily average vehicle flow and speed were $34,818 \\pm 2,214 \\mathrm{veh} \\cdot \\mathrm{h}^{-1}$ and $33.47 \\pm 7.95 \\mathrm{km} \\cdot \\mathrm{h}^{-1}$ on weekday and were $30,818 \\pm 1,848 \\mathrm{veh} \\cdot \\mathrm{h}^{-1}$ and $33.86 \\pm 8.83 \\mathrm{km} \\cdot \\mathrm{h}^{-1}$ on weekend. Vehicle flows in peak hour were $2,173 \\pm 161$ and $1,952 \\pm 68 \\mathrm{veh} \\cdot \\mathrm{h}^{-1}$ on weekday and weekend, respectively. East Yan'an Road tunnel was located in the city center of Shanghai, where heavy-duty diesel vehicles (HDDVs) were restricted to access except for some medium-duty diesel vehicles (MDDVs). Therefore, fleet in the tunnel was mainly comprised of light-duty gasoline vehicles, which accounted for $93.7 \\pm 2.2\\%$ and $94.6 \\pm 1.9\\%$ of the total on weekday and weekend, respectively. The", "tunnel in Taiwan in 2006. The CO, $\\mathrm{NO}_x$ , and $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ EFs were $0.91 \\pm 0.47 - 1.47 \\pm 0.63$ , $0.15 \\pm 0.07 - 0.33 \\pm 0.17\\mathrm{gkm}^{-1} \\cdot \\mathrm{veh}^{-1}$ , and $2 \\pm 2 - 4 \\pm 3\\mathrm{mgkm}^{-1} \\cdot \\mathrm{veh}^{-1}$ , respectively. Their results were the lowest compared with other tunnel studies in China mainly because Taiwan implemented Tier 1 and Tier 2 emission standards in 1999 and 2008, earlier than the mainland of China. Liu et al. (2015a,b) and Deng et al. (2015) measured CO and $\\mathrm{PM}_{10}$ EFs in East Yan'an Road tunnel in 2012. CO EF decreased by about $30\\%$ compared with our result in 2016. $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ EF also declined if we considered $\\mathrm{PM}_{10}$ was similar to $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ for vehicle exhaust. The enforcement of vehicle emission control strategies should be the main reason for the decrease. Between 2012 and 2016, Shanghai implemented the emission standards of LDGVs from Euro 4 to Euro 5, eliminated Euro 0 gasoline vehicles from the whole city, and restricted Euro 1 gasoline vehicles in urban area. CO and $\\mathrm{NO}_x$ EFs in Yingpan Road tunnel were $0.75 \\pm 0.56 - 6.05 \\pm 5.94$ and $0.12 \\pm 0.02 - 0.82 \\pm 0.76\\mathrm{gkm}^{-1} \\cdot \\mathrm{veh}^{-1}$ (Deng et al., 2015). Our results were relatively lower than the average value of Yingpan Road tunnel. The fractions of gasoline and diesel vehicles have influences on EFs in the tunnels. CO, $\\mathrm{NO}_x$ , and $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ EFs in Zhujiang tunnel were much higher than those in our study since there was higher proportion of diesel vehicles (Liu et al., 2014; Zhang et al., 2015b). Correspondingly, KXL tunnel had lower $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ EF due to its high proportion of gasoline vehicles (Cui et al., 2016).", "

content.title: Fig. 2. Comparisons of emission uncertainties of coal-fired power plants in Guizhou in 2005 using sector-based and unit-based methods. The “+” represents the central value and the box represents the  95\%  CI using the unit-based method, while the larger range line represents the  95\%  CI using the sector-based method.
content.summary: Figure 2 compares emissions uncertainties of coal-fired power plants in Guizhou, 2005, using sector-based and unit-based methods. Central values and 95% CIs are shown for each pollutant.
content.insight: The figure demonstrates that using a unit-based method reduces emission uncertainty compared to the sector-based approach, highlighting the importance of detailed source information.

metadata.full_json: {"id": "img_189", "source_md": "Quantifying the uncertainties of a bottom-up emiss.md", "save_path": "C:\\liuxingyu\\multisubspace-data\\predataprocess\\paper_md\\Quantifying the uncertainties of a bottom-up emiss\\figure_2.jpg", "title": "Fig. 2. Comparisons of emission uncertainties of coal-fired power plants in Guizhou in 2005 using sector-based and unit-based methods. The “+” represents the central value and the box represents the  95\\%  CI using the unit-based method, while the larger range line represents the  95\\%  CI using the sector-based method.", "references": ["In this study, such a test is conducted for one province in China as an example, Guizhou, where coal with extremely high sulfur content is used in power generation. As shown in Fig. 2, the  $95\\%$  CIs using the sector-based method is largest for  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ $(-30\\% \\sim 108\\%)$  and smallest for  $\\mathrm{NO}_x$ $(-25\\% \\sim 20\\%)$ . Compared to  $\\mathrm{NO}_x$ , the  $95\\%$  CI of  $\\mathrm{SO}_2$  emission is relatively large, reaching  $-40\\% \\sim 42\\%$ , and the variation of sulfur contents is estimated to contribute almost  $90\\%$  to that uncertainty. Employing a unit-based method, the  $95\\%$  CIs for all species are significantly reduced, with the largest ranging  $-9\\% \\sim 18\\%$ , for  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  and the smallest  $-8\\% \\sim 8\\%$ , for  $\\mathrm{SO}_2$ . Figure 3 shows the difference in distributions of  $\\mathrm{SO}_2$  emissions applying the two methods. The test confirms that the uncertainties of provincial and national emissions from power plants may be larger using a sector-based method compared to a unit-based one. Lacking detailed source information, however, such judgment cannot be easily extrapolated to other sectors without similar tests."], "functional_roles": {"main_role": "Results & Findings", "sub_roles": ["Compare Experimental Performance"]}, "visual_type": {"main_type": "Quantitative Charts", "sub_types": ["Box Plot"]}, "key_entities": ["emission uncertainties", "coal-fired power plants", "Guizhou", "sector-based methods", "unit-based methods", "SO2", "NOx", "PM2.5"], "concise_summary": "Figure 2 compares emissions uncertainties of coal-fired power plants in Guizhou, 2005, using sector-based and unit-based methods. content.title: Fig. 3. The analysis framework of the sources for energy increase in the passenger transport sector in China.
content.summary: The diagram outlines the sources of energy increase in China's passenger transport sector, detailing sub-sectors and influential factors like population and vehicle stock structure.
content.insight: The framework highlights the impact of policy measures on energy efficiency, emphasizing transportation structure adjustment and fuel economy improvements for significant energy savings.

metadata.full_json:
{"id": "img_74", "source_md": "Energy growth sources and future energy-saving pot.md", "save_path": "C:\liuxingyu\multisubspace-data\predataprocess\paper_md\Energy growth sources and future energy-saving pot\figure_3.jpg", "title": "Fig. 3. The analysis framework of the sources for energy increase in the passenger transport sector in China.", "references": ["analysis framework is proposed to consider different characteristics of traffic modes, as shown in Fig. 3. The effects of main driving factors on total passenger transport energy use are analyzed briefly. Furthermore, a specific investigation into three subsectors of passenger transport is performed in turn.", "# Energy growth sources and future energy-saving potentials in passenger transportation sector in China", "In China, energy use from passenger vehicles is increasing rapidly due to urbanization and income growth, but the contributions of transport service structure shift and energy efficiency improvement, especially future energy-saving directions are controversial and unclear because of the differences in the choices of traffic modes, which may greatly affect the analysis results and their interpretations. This study investigates the growth sources of energy use in the passenger transport sector during 1979-2017 and future energy-saving potentials by 2030 by re-examining statistical data. Results show that the transport service increase and transport structure shift led to the increase in energy use by  $665.25\%$  and  $337.22\%$ , but approximately  $14\%$  of the increased energy use was offset by energy efficiency improvements, and its effect is limited in public transportation sector, compared with private passenger vehicle sector. In public passenger transport sector, adjusting the service or volume structure may be a desirable option while in private passenger vehicle sector the policy focus is more on improving fuel efficiency and reducing average annual kilometers traveled per capita. Overall, there is still at least  $28.3\%$  of energy reduction potential by energy efficiency improvement before 2030 compared to 2017 baseline level. Potential evaluation showed that the current potential of transport service structure adjustment based on industrial planning is limited, which highlights the necessity of reform, involving promoting the sharing transportation, railway, subway and buses.", "In the last two decades, the transport sector has become a major source of energy consumption worldwide. In China, transport energy use increased from 58.63 Mtce (million tons of coal equivalent) in 1995 to 421.91 Mtce in 2017, with an annual average growth rate of nearly  $10\%$ . Energy use from passenger transport dominates total transport energy use, accounting for nearly  $60\%$  in 2017. By 2030, more than 300 million new rural residents are expected to enter the cities in China, which will have a great impact on the passenger transport energy consumption. Passengers are relatively easily guided by policy measures to change their energy-using behaviors in their daily travel, thus leading to low-carbon", "However, one of the key challenges is that the current energy statistics of passenger transportation are fragmented in China. The lack of comprehensive statistic specification causes that the data is scattered into different sectors in energy statistical system. The data does not properly cover metrics of interest for energy consumption analysis of passenger transportation. Part of the data is covered in the Transport, Storage and Post sector and Residential Consumption sector in China Energy Statistical Yearbook [3]. The former only includes the intercity public passenger transport and", "Many studies have investigated energy consumption and emissions increase in transport sector [2,5,6]. The relationship between transport energy use and socio-economic factors has also been intensively discussed using econometric models [7-11]. Various options to save energy and reduce emissions in the transport sector have been evaluated for their reduction potential, from both the technological and behavioral perspectives [1,12,13]. In transport studies, bottom-up methodology and scenario analysis were often used to study future trends, mitigation options, and reduction potential [14,15]. In particular, road transport and passenger transport have always been the hot topics for evaluating energy consumption and emissions [16,17].", "Either from an economy-wide or a sectoral perspective, the driving forces of increased energy consumption have been extensively investigated [18-20]. In the transportation sector, these studies could be summarized from two perspectives, namely, 1) the sectoral perspective, and 2) the vehicle-based perspective. From the sectoral perspective, changes in energy consumption and emission have been analyzed either for the whole transportation sector or its subsectors. Activity factors, namely Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth, population increase, and propensity to travel, were often found to be the main drivers of growing energy consumption [21-24], which is similar to the passenger transport subsector [25-27]. Other studies have focused more on micro sections of transportation. For example, on the vehicle level, the vehicle population, fuel mix, and vehicle fuel efficiency were", "With accelerating urbanization in China, passenger transport energy use has rapidly increased, from 28.78 Mtce in 1997 to 252.47 Mtce in 2017, with an annual average growth rate of  $11.47\%$  (Fig. 1). Energy use could thus be divided into three development stages. From 1978 to 1990, the intercity public passenger transport was the main traveling mode of passengers, as the urban and the private vehicle subsectors were undeveloped. From 1990 to 2002, energy consumption of the private passenger vehicles gradually caught up with that of the intercity subsector. From 2003 to 2017, the private subsector became the major source of the passenger transport energy use. The energy intensity of passenger transport decreased continually due to technological advances, and the energy intensity of the private passenger vehicles decreased rapidly from 143.68 Tce/Mp-km (ton coal equivalent/million passenger-kilometers) in 1997 to 51.38 Tce/Mp-km in 2017, due to the policy of fuel consumption limits for passenger cars [48]. This corresponds to a huge reduction in fuel consumption. Without this reduction in energy intensity, the fuel consumption in the private passenger transport sector would have increased nearly three times greater than it is today.", "(c) Modal energy intensity  
Fig. 7. Energy consumption and passenger volume share of three modes of the urban public passenger transport, 1978-2017. Data sources: China Statistical Yearbook [4]; China Urban Construction Statistical Yearbook [69]; Statistics bulletin of transportation sector development [64]; Fuel consumption limits for passenger cars, Fuel consumption evaluation methods and targets for passenger cars [48] and the authors' personal calculation.", "The data includes socio-economic activity, transport structure and energy intensity. The socio-economic activity data includes the population, the number of households and the GDP, which are from the China Statistical Yearbook [4]; the transport-related activity data includes the passenger service of the intercity public passenger transport, the passenger volume of the urban public passenger transport and the vehicle population of the private passenger vehicles, which are from 1) Passenger Kilometers of the Transport, Postal and Telecommunication Services, and Software Industry, 2) Basic Statistics on Public Transportation in Cities by Region of the Urban, Rural and Regional Development, 3) Possession of Private Vehicles of the Transport, Postal and Telecommunication Services, and Software Industry in the China Statistical Yearbook [4]. The structure data consists of the service structure of the intercity public modes, the volume structure of the urban public modes and the vehicle type structure of the private subsector, which are calculated based on the above transport-related activity data. The intensity data includes the energy intensity of the three subsectors and the average annual mileage of the private passenger vehicles, which are from the Yearbook of China Integrated transport [63], the Statistics bulletin of transportation sector development [64], the Railway Statistics [65], and the Fuel consumption limits for passenger cars [48]. The average annual mileage of the private passenger vehicles is from the survey data in the FEEI model [49].", "The GDP per capita effect was the dominant contributor to energy consumption growth and its contribution increased over time, but the contribution decreased during 1996-2000 (Figs. 5b) and 2008-2010 (Fig. 5c) due to the Asian financial crisis and the global financial crisis respectively. The shift of various modes of passenger transport (structure effect) contributed to the energy consumption decrease in the years leading up to 1990 because the passenger service share of civil aviation decreased (Fig. 5c). Since 1990, the structure effect turned from a negative value to a positive value. After 1990, the highway and civil aviation services were promoted vigorously by the State Council and Civil Aviation Administration of China [50,66]. Therefore, adjusting the transport structure in the intercity subsector, such as promoting the transfer form highway and civil aviation to railway, could be an effective way to save energy. The passenger service intensity effect provides an increasingly negative contribution to the increase in energy consumption during 1978-2017. The energy intensity of the railway, waterway, and civil aviation services decreased rapidly due to technological advancements and improved management.", "The GDP per capita effect was the dominant contributor to energy consumption growth due to rapid economic development in China. The most prominent contribution appeared during 1991-1995 and 2006-2010, with an energy consumption increase of  $275\%$  and  $195\%$ , respectively (Fig. 6b). The passenger volume structure effect was another main driver of the energy consumption growth due to the shift in the dominant mode of transport. From the late 1980s to the 1990s, the taxi industry grew rapidly, owing to the policy of open business implemented by the State Council", "The multiplication decomposition results in the three subsectors are shown in Fig. 9. Five influential factors on energy consumption can be observed and are quite similar in their effects in the intercity and urban passenger transport subsectors. The GDP per capita increase was always the dominant contributor to rises in energy consumption, but this effect was larger in the urban subsector (2.713). The passenger volume/service per GDP played a critical role in reducing energy consumption as opposed to energy intensity in the public transportation sector. In contrast, however, the private transport subsector showed that fuel efficiency improvement, decline of family size and average annual kilometers per capita offset the effect of vehicle stock growth per capita together.", "This study contributes to understanding the underlying driving factors of energy growth and exploring energy efficiency improvement opportunities in passenger transport sector in China. First, a comprehensive picture of passenger transport energy consumption trends during 1979-2017 in China is given. Main drivers of energy growth are briefly identified through re-classifying the fragmented data statistics, which, to our knowledge, are unique and original. Second, the effects of the main driving forces of increased energy consumption are investigated firstly by using a hybrid decomposition method. Third, the future energy-saving potentials of different policy pathways until 2020 and 2030 are estimated and compared involving sharing transportation, structure adjustment, energy efficiency improvement, and urban car ownership control.", "The results show that there are diversified contributors to energy consumption depending on the traffic modes utilized. On the whole, GDP growth per capita, motorization level and shifts in service structure mainly contribute to energy consumption increase in passenger transport sector during the period 1979-2017, and they cause more than 10 times increase in energy consumption, but the actual energy consumption increased just by 8.77 times because of the effect of energy efficiency improvement. For the intercity and urban public passenger transport, the GDP growth per capita, the service or volume structure change, and population growth strongly dominate the changes in energy consumption, and the effect of energy efficiency improvement is not very significant on a relative basis. In the private passenger vehicle sector, however, the situation is significantly different. Fuel efficiency improvement, decline of family size and average annual kilometers traveled per capita together offset the positive impacts from motorization level rise and vehicle type structure change. Especially, the fuel efficiency improvement has enormously contributed to energy conservation since 2010 in China.", "Outlook analysis shows that by 2030 energy conservation potential in intercity public passenger transport sector is limited (16.6%), but there are still huge energy-saving potentials in urban public passenger transport sector (54.3%) and private passenger transport sector (34.3%). They could totally lead to 31.6% of energy consumption reduction in passenger transport sector by 2030.", "In order to reduce energy use in passenger transport sector, multiple policy directions deserve attention. In China, the motorization level could be regulated by some mandatory policies, such as the vehicle license lottery in major cities, such as Beijing and Tianjin. In future, it is essential to give priority to public transport and sharing transport, such as the subway, bus, car-sharing, and bike-sharing. Particularly, first, sharing transportation deserves more attention in China because Chinese people are more likely to accept sharing and collective ideas, and there are less regulatory restrictions in China. Second, the energy intensity reduction targets up until 2020 need to be strictly enforced, especially for fuel efficiency standards for private passenger vehicles. Third, low-intensity transport modes, such as railway, waterway, rail transit, and mini vehicles, are strongly recommended to promote the low-carbon transition.", "[1] Waisman HD, Guivarch C, Lecocq F. img_74.source_reason: 中国的空气污染现状不仅涉及其程度，还涉及其来源和治理措施。这个方向可能会揭示更多关于空气污染的成因和治理策略的科学研究。

evaluation.extracted_insight: 分析框架指出政策措施对能效的影响，强调了交通结构调整和燃油经济性改善对显著的能源节约的重要性。
evaluation.reason: 尽管内容与能源使用有关，但更像是背景资料，未直接与研究方向强相关。

content.title: Fig. 4. Comparisons of annual emissions for China from different studies. The emissions are for year 2005 unless noted. The range line represents the  95\%  CIs around the central estimates of this study.
content.summary: Figure 4 compares annual emissions for China in 2005 from various studies, showing central estimates and 95% confidence intervals for each emission type.
content.insight: The figure highlights the consistency of this study's emissions estimates with other studies, except for some outliers. It emphasizes the role of interannual variability in explaining certain discrepancies.

They estimated that the uncertainties of Chinese emissions varied from  $-12\\% \\sim 13\\%$  for  $\\mathrm{SO}_2$  to  $-83\\% \\sim 495\\%$  for organic carbon (expressed as the lower and upper bounds of a  $95\\%$  confidence interval, CI, around a central estimate). content.title: Fig. 8. Correlation coefficients between air quality change and socioeconomic factors.
content.summary: Fig. 8 shows correlation coefficients between air quality change and socioeconomic factors, indicating low multicollinearity and relationships among the variables.
content.insight: The figure highlights that socioeconomic factors, such as environmental governance, play a key role in air quality improvement, with no high correlations reducing reliability concerns of the analysis.
